title: ML-Cloud Computing of SST and Mobile Application Development for Remote Monitoring of OC-OTEC Plant
authors: S. Ashwanth, Vijay Venkatesh. G, M. Sanjay Kumar, S. Sutha, Biren Pattanaik
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Renewable energy plays a crucial role in supplying power and freshwater to remote island regions, and among the available options, Ocean Thermal Energy Conversion (OTEC) stands out as an efficient and sustainable approach to energy generation. Several operational parameters govern the performance of an Open-Cycle OTEC (OC-OTEC) system, including temperature gradient between warm water and cold water, mass flow rate of warm water, mass flow rate of cold water, turbine speed, flow rate of fresh water and turbine speed etc. Further, temperature gradient is highly influencing OTEC performance and leads to component failure due to Sea Surface Temperature (SST) variation under climate changes. Hence continuous monitoring of SST and OTEC plant parameters are essential for proper control action. In this paper, SST is predicted based on ECMWF historical SST dataset using Long Short Term Memory(LSTM) algorithm and OC-OTEC parameters are monitored through cloud computing and those parameters are remotely monitored through mobile application. The proposed methodology integrates an LSTM-based SST forecasting model trained on historical datasets with a cloud-enabled monitoring framework, in which all OC-OTEC plant parameters are acquired, stored, and displayed through a dedicated OTEC Mobile Application for remote access. The results show the LSTM model predicts SST with MSE and accuracy of 0.1046 and 98.5%. This mobile application can be used to monitor the plant parameters of OC-OTEC established by National Institute of Ocean Technology Chennai at Kavaratti Lakshadweep remotely.

title: An Efficient CNN with Logistic Regression Model for Detecting and Predicting the Severity of Bike Dents
authors: Raghavendra Sridhar, Rahil Sharma, Rashi Nimesh Kumar Dhenia, Ishva Jitendrakumar Kanani, Jhulan Kumar, Gaurav Sharma, Deepak Banerjee
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: The severity of bike dents can be efficiently identified and categorized in the ever-changing landscape of intelligent transportation systems, which is paramount to more reliable inspection and less errors made by humans. In this paper, a hybrid architecture may be proposed that integrates the Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to extract features automatically and the Logistic Regression to classify the severity of dent with multiple classes. Based on the experience of new developments in the fields of dent detection, localization of damage and transfer learning methodologies, the proposed system classifies dents in five severity levels. To train and evaluate the model, a full dataset, which was enhanced and preprocessed to become robust, was used. Large experiments showed high performance with a consistency of 95 and 96 accuracy, recall and F1-score, and classification accuracy of 98.17-98.47 over all the severity levels. These findings prove the possibility of the hybrid model to be implemented in real-life scenarios that include automated bike services, insurance claims system, and mobile inspection devices.

title: AI-Powered Exception Management in Retail Planning Systems
authors: Lukesh Singla, Manthan Lad
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The intricate interdependencies of contemporary supply chain dynamics are difficult for conventional rule-based systems to handle, which is a major issue in retail planning systems. In this study, we investigate the impact of AI methods for root cause attribution and exception detection in retail planning settings. In terms of exception identification, machine learning algorithms perform better than traditional threshold methods, according to an analysis of 5,200 retail planning situations. Support Vector Machines had an exception detection accuracy of 99.04 %. Additionally, mathematical models for anomaly scoring were created, and we used a variety of predicted features, including supplier performance metrics, inventory fluctuation, and demand variance, as proof. The findings demonstrate that AI-based techniques can use ensemble methods to detect exceptions with a sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 5 6 \%}$</tex> and minimize false positive rates by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 3 \%}$</tex>.

title: Research on Reaction Methods for Oil Pump Controller CAN Communication Loss
authors: Erpeng Wang, Yuxuan Jiang, Yang Gao, Xiaokui Huang, Jun Fu, Jian Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: In modern automotive systems, Controller Area Network (CAN) communication plays a crucial role in ensuring the reliable operation of various components, including oil pumps. However, CAN communication loss can lead to critical system failures, particularly in controlling oil pump speeds. This paper proposes an innovative method to address the issue of oil pump control when CAN communication fails. By utilizing wake-up wiring harness signal to transmit frequency-based control signals, this approach ensures continued oil pump operation even in the absence of CAN communication. The proposed method detects communication faults, converts target speeds into frequency signals, and enables the oil pump controller to adjust pump speed based on these frequencies. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness and reliability of this method, offering a robust solution for maintaining oil pump control under communication failure conditions.

title: Deep Residual Feature-Enhanced DenseNet for Automated Age-related Macular Degeneration Detection in OCT Images
authors: C. Valvilori., Nithishkumar R, Ajay K
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This work proposes an optimized deep learning framework for the automated identification of Age-related Macular Degeneration (AMD) using DenseNet-121 from Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) images. The approach undergoes a thorough preprocessing pipeline: grayscale conversion, resizing, and denoising; this is done to enhance the quality of images for the extraction of robust features. This model utilizes the pre-trained architecture of DenseNet-121 and finetunes it to classify OCT images as either normal or abnormal with very high accuracy. This addresses one of the biggest challenges in the early detection of AMD. Another novelty of this work is the inclusion of an end-to-end deployment using Streamlit, allowing for real-time predictions on uploaded OCT images without requiring further retraining; thus, it bridges the gap between work and clinical usability. Experimental evaluations have been performed which show that the model reaches efficient convergence during training and is reliable on unseen data. This work underlines a practical and scalable solution for ophthalmic image analysis, joining advanced deep learning techniques with an interactive interface for immediate diagnostic support.

title: Lightweight Blockchain-Based Framework for Credit Risk Assessment and Experimental Analysis
authors: Jiayi Tian, Chuan Li
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Traditional centralized credit risk assessment systems suffer from limited transparency and vulnerability to tampering, which undermines the reliability of financial data management. To address this issue, this paper proposes a lightweight blockchain-based framework that uses a hash-based storage and verification mechanism to achieve decentralized integrity protection for key credit data, while integrating logistic regression and random forest models for credit risk prediction. Experimental results on the UCI credit card dataset show that the proposed framework maintains high prediction accuracy and significantly reduces blockchain storage and operational overhead, demonstrating an effective balance between security, efficiency, and model performance.

title: Research on ROI Partition Design Based on Expert Prior Knowledge and ESGCN - Driven Flexible AU Recognition Network Model
authors: Zhenzhou Wang, Mengze Zhao, Shao Cui, Shuxian Fan
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper focuses on the preprocessing of facial feature points and conducts research. Firstly, based on expert prior knowledge, ROI (Region of Interest) partitions were designed to lay the foundation for subsequent facial-related analysis; then, the design of the flexible AU (Action Unit) recognition network model was focused on, and a complete research framework including the system model and problem description, specific solutions, simulation results and discussion, and conclusion was constructed. The paper first elaborates on the research motivation, summarizes the related work background, and clarifies the research direction; then, it introduces the structure of the system model in detail, clearly expounds the essence of the problem and the corresponding solutions; subsequently, through simulation experiments, results are obtained and in-depth discussions are carried out to verify the effectiveness of the scheme; finally, the research results are summarized, forming a complete research system for flexible AU recognition network model, providing a valuable technical solution and research idea for the field of facial action unit recognition.

title: Depth of Reinforcement Learning Optimization of Low Traffic Conflict Resolution Model
authors: Lei Liu, Xuefeng Shi, Fan Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: With the accelerated process of opening up low-altitude airspace, the large-scale application of new types of aircraft such as unmanned aerial vehicles and flying cars has led to the airspace system presenting characteristics of high density, strong dynamics and heterogeneity. Traditional air traffic control strategies based on fixed rules are difficult to meet the demands of real-time conflict resolution. In view of the limitations of existing methods in three-dimensional spatio-temporal feature modeling, dynamic interaction capture and system scalability, this paper proposes a spatio-temporal collaborative graph reinforcement learning model (STC-GRLM). This model captures the trajectory interaction relationship in real time through a dynamic spatio-temporal graph structure and reconstructs the conflict topology with a dual-threshold mechanism, achieving a paradigm leap from passive detection to active decision-making. The third-order reward function is designed to integrate the goals of safety, timeliness and stability. The traditional boundary detection logic is replaced by the exponential risk penalty, and the long-term path planning is optimized by combining the Gaussian kernel timeliness reward. The decoupling of computing load is achieved by adopting a light quantum graph decision-maker, and the bottleneck of centralized model expansion is broken through through a local observation and incremental synchronization mechanism. Experiments show that this model significantly improves the efficiency of conflict resolution and the robustness of the system, and still maintains high collaborative performance in complex disturbance environments.

title: Integrating an Innovative Named Entity Recognition Algorithm Within Knowledge Engineering Frameworks
authors: Yan Li
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This study developed a complete system based on the BERT model to be applied in programming courses of engineering and science at higher education institutions, thereby meeting and adapting to the growing demand for artificial intelligence literacy in the field of HE (Higher Education). Based on the traditional BERT model, this study proposed a new model innovated by named entity recognition. A major innovation of this study is that it independently constructed a question-and-answer dataset based on student learning data and conducted experiments and analysis on this self-built dataset, with results indicating that this model is more effective than classic models. Another significant innovation of this study is the development of a question-and-answer system based on both the teacher and the student sides, aimed at helping teachers and students further strengthen and understand common issues in programming learning. The new model, which combines innovations in named entity recognition technology, was experimented on and analyzed using the self-built question-and-answer dataset, showing that this model is more efficient than traditional models. In the case study comparison based on two universities, the system was confirmed to facilitate autonomous learning during the programming learning process and to infer personalized learning plans suitable for students based on learning paths. After the analysis of the experiment, a majority of the teachers and students agreed that this system could improve the efficiency of programming learning to some extent.

title: Enhancing Breast Cancer Classification Through GAN-Generated Synthetic Malignant Samples: A Study on the UCI Wisconsin Dataset
authors: Keshav Balakrishna, Aditya Baisakh, Vivan Kanna, Aaron Lee, Moemal Al-Wishah
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: The inherent class imbalance in breast cancer diagnostic datasets limits the generalizability and accuracy of ML models, often resulting in the under-detection of malignant instances. In this study, a minority class augmentation approach for tabular clinical data − through a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) trained exclusively on malignant samples − to generate high-fidelity synthetic minority instances while avoiding noise from majority class features. The augmented data are then used to balance the training set for a soft-voting ensemble classifier. Using 5-fold stratified cross-validation, the proposed model achieved an average validation accuracy of 98.07% and F1-score of 97.40%, with low standard deviations indicating robustness. Comparative analysis against recent augmentation frameworks shows competitive or superior results, supporting the effectiveness of targeted GAN-based augmentation for robust breast cancer classification.

title: Design and Application of Automatic Detection Technology for Cigarette Nozzle Spray Performance
authors: Jianfeng Chen, Jiamin Lei, Ziyang Zeng, Wenyu Liao, Liming Lin, Yingxiang Wu, Xianpei Lin, Huanchao Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: In cigarette manufacturing, nozzle calibration traditionally relies on manual expertise. This approach proves inefficient, yields inconsistent results, and lacks effective feedback mechanisms. To address these challenges, we developed an automated nozzle detection and calibration system utilizing colorimetric materials to convert spray coverage into visual data. Industrial cameras capture these images, which are processed through algorithms to calculate key metrics like coverage rate and uniformity. The system estimates droplet size via calibration curves and adjusts liquid supply pressure or valve openings through control feedback. Comparative experiments conducted during flavoring and material addition processes at a cigarette factory demonstrated that the automated system achieves rapid, stable, and quantitative evaluation of nozzle atomization performance. This breakthrough not only significantly enhances calibration efficiency but also improves product consistency. The research provides a low-cost, user-friendly solution for nozzle quality control in the tobacco industry, while offering valuable references for spray quality management in food processing, pharmaceuticals, and agricultural applications.

title: SCR-YOLO: An Efficient UAV Object Detection Framework Based on Spatial-Channel Reconstruction and Statistical Metric Learning
authors: Xiuquan Yue, Tianshi Zhang, Hanyu Deng, Shiwei Wang, Junying Yuan, Haishan Chen, Haoran Deng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems (AISNS)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) imagery has emerged as a pivotal data source for intelligent social governance applications, such as urban traffic monitoring and crowd analysis. However, object detection in high-resolution aerial views faces severe challenges, particularly for tiny targets (e.g., pedestrians and vehicles). Most detectors suffer from computational redundancy in irrelevant background regions and gradient instability when localizing discrete tiny objects, primarily due to extreme background noise and the limitations of traditional geometric metrics in handling minute scale variations. To address these methodological and mathematical challenges, this paper proposes SCR-YOLO, an efficient detection framework based on structural optimization of YOLOv8. First, we design a novel Spatial-Channel Reconstruction C2f (SCR-C2f) module by integrating SCConv to replace the second standard convolution in the bottleneck unit. By integrating a redundancy-reducing topology, SCR-C2f effectively suppresses substantial background noise and refines the features of sparse target entities. Second, we introduce a collaborative regression strategy integrating Normalized Gaussian Wasserstein Distance (NWD). By modeling bounding boxes as 2D Gaussian distributions, this statistical metric mitigates the sensitivity and instability issues inherent in traditional IoU-based geometric metrics when processing tiny objects. Experimental results on the challenging VisDrone2019 benchmark demonstrate that our method significantly improves mAP50 by 5.6% and mAP50–95 by 1.2%, reaching 30.9% and 16.8%, respectively,with a real-time inference speed of 115 FPS on an NVIDIA RTX A4000 GPU. Consequently, the proposed method holds significant potential for deployment in intelligent social governance systems and resource-constrained UAV edge networks.

title: Analysis of Word-Line Underdrive Effects on FinFET-Based 6T SRAM Cells Across Different Device Configurations
authors: Indervir Singh, Jobandeep Singh, Tejpartap Singh, Krishna Gopal Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Signal Processing and Electronics Design (ICSPED)
abstract: Continuous technology scaling in SRAMs has resulted in lower supply voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DD</inf>) and device dimensions, adversely affecting the cell stability and noise margins. This work investigates the impact of Word-line Underdrive (WLUD) on the Read Static Noise Margin (RSNM) of FinFET-based 6T SRAM cells for multiple device configurations. The RSNM is evaluated for word-line voltages (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">WL</inf>) ranging from 800 mV to 400 mV, across configurations with varied pull-down (PD) and pass-gate (PG) transistor strengths, using DC butterfly-curve analysis. WLUD improves RSNM for all device configurations, though the improvement reduces with an increase in Cell Ratio (CR). Under strong underdrive, cells with smaller CR achieve higher RSNM, enabling area-efficient SRAM operation. For a fixed CR, cells with weaker PG transistors show greater read stability, indicating that PG transistors exert a stronger influence on RSNM than PD transistors.

title: Research on Regression Model of Fire Occurrence Probability Based on Bayesian Theorem for Meteorological Data
authors: Xiaochuan Wu, Zhenpeng An, Yitian Qiu, Di Zhang
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Exploring the regression relationship between meteorological data and fire occurrence has always been a research hotspot in the field of fire risk analysis. Based on hourly meteorological data, this paper collected and organized 3,910 fire data throughout the year of 2023 in a certain area, as well as a total of 8,760 hourly data of temperature, humidity, air pressure, precipitation, ground wind speed and wind direction throughout the year. By using the basic statistical data sorted out through the Bayesian formula and combining meteorological elements such as temperature, humidity and wind speed, researchers established curve models and multivariate linear models to quantitatively explain the relationship between these meteorological elements and urban fire occurrence.

title: An AI-Enhanced Metalens Array: Co-Design of Nanophotonics and Deep Learning for Wide-Angle Imaging
authors: Zi-Wen Zhou, Zhongyi Yuan, Zaichen Zhang, Ji Chen
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Metalens array design represents an effective approach to achieving wide-angle imaging, yet it inevitably suffers from severe off-axis aberrations, manifested as both geometric distortion and resolution loss. Although traditional image processing can effectively correct the geometric distortion, it fails to recover the lost resolution and fine details. To overcome this fundamental limitation, we propose an AI-enhanced imaging strategy tailored for metalens arrays. After a conventional nonlinear distortion correction step, a dedicated Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) is employed to perform intelligent restoration of the resolved but blurry sub-images. This GAN is specifically trained to address the position-dependent blurring inherent to the metalens array. The improvements are substantial: our AI restoration achieves a dramatic improvement of up to 37.0% in PSNR and 35.1% in SSIM, effectively recovering textures and edges that are irrecoverable by conventional methods. This work highlights a distinct division of roles where AI excels at solving the inverse problem of detail restoration, presenting a compelling pathway for AI in overcoming the persistent challenges of advanced optical systems.

title: Machine Learning-Based Early Detection of Autism Spectrum Disorder in Children: A Predictive Analytical Approach
authors: Hafiz Arslan Ramzan, Kamrul Islam, MD Ahbab Hussain, Asif Khan, Ahmad Ali Javed, Sadia Ramzan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: Early screening of Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is essential for timely intervention and improved developmental outcomes. This study presents a machine learning-based approach for classifying ASD cases in children using behavioral and demographic screening data. A dataset from the UCI repository, comprising 292 child instances with 21 features, was analyzed. The pipeline involved robust preprocessing, Principal Component Analysis (PCA), and model training using Logistic Regression and Random Forest classifiers. To prevent data leakage and overfitting, all steps were conducted within a cross-validated pipeline. Evaluation metrics were derived using 5-fold cross-validation, yielding average accuracies of 92.6% and 94.1%, respectively. Key predictors included behavioral features (A5, A9, A10) and family history. The results indicate the potential of machine learning for assisting ASD screening but emphasize the need for rigorous validation, generalizability testing, and careful clinical deployment.

title: A Comparative Study of Hybrid Deep Learning and Bio-Inspired Optimization Techniques for Energyefficient Routing in Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Sankara Rao Allada, Manoj Kumar Nigam
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Recent developments in wireless sensor networks (WSNs) have led to significant advances in energy-efficient routing protocols through the integration of deep learning and bio-inspired optimization algorithms. WSNs face critical challenges due to limited battery capacity, computational constraints, and dynamic network topology. This review article provides a comprehensive comparative analysis of state-of-the-art approaches for energy optimization in WSNs, focusing on clustering and routing protocols. We examine three major publications that propose hybrid frameworks combining deep learning architectures (CapsNet, ResNet-50) with metaheuristic optimization algorithms (ETSA, SOPSO, ACO). The analysis categorizes these protocols based on network architecture, energy models, optimization strategies, and performance metrics. We compare their effectiveness in extending network lifetime, reducing energy consumption, improving packet delivery ratio, and minimizing transmission delay. The comparative study demonstrates that hybrid deep learning-swarm intelligence approaches achieve 18-38% improvement in network lifetime, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 4 \%}$</tex> reduction in energy consumption, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{2 4 \%}$</tex> lower delay compared to traditional protocols. This article serves as a comprehensive reference for researchers and practitioners working on nextgeneration energy-efficient WSN deployments.

title: Potato Leaf Disease Detection in Agriculture: Bridging the Gap with Deep Learning Approaches
authors: Devika Kshatriya, Kamal Sutaria, Saurabh Shah
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Potato is the world's fourth most common staple food crop and thus plays a key role in food security and dietary diversity. However, the tuber industry is impacted by many leaf diseases, which in Potato include fungal, bacterial and viral infections that greatly affect productivity and inevitably create financial loss for farmers. Historically, traditional methods of disease detection such as stand counting and classical digital image processing techniques have failed due to subjectivity, subjectivity of human interpretation, labor, and the lack of scale under real-world farming conditions. In the past few years, deep learning has become one of the most flexible options, as it provides automated feature extraction and is largely accurate, and offers the possibility of implementing real-time detection. This review systematically discusses current progress and trends in deep learning for leaf disease detection in potato, outlining the various models, benchmark datasets and key findings across studies. We compare research studies to their dedicated strengths and weaknesses, along with putting forward a critique of those significant issues such as dataset balance, limited generalization to field-settings, and interpretability. Lastly, we determine and offer future directions for research, such as lightweight and explainable models, practical application of these models within IoT-based platforms and collaborative multi-disciplinary focused work. We hope that the insights provided in this review will guide both the research world and the practitioners to support decision-making and the development of scalable and farmer-facing leaf disease detection systems.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Progression-based Crop Disease Detection with Hyperspectral Imaging
authors: Shreshtha Singh, Garima Jaiswal, Arun Sharma
publish: 2025 7th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Speech Technology (AIST)
abstract: Precision Agriculture is the phenomenon of data-based management of crops ensuring resource sustainability and optimization. In order to enhance management level decision making, Precision Agriculture utilizes Hyperspectral Imaging which provides a potent method to detect unseen changes in a crop’s health in a non-invasive manner. By detecting menial shifts in spectral and temporal ranges HSI can also be utilized to perform stage specific vigilance over crop health. This study employs a framework to detect stage specific diseases in various crops. A diverse dataset comprising six crop health stages was analyzed using various machine learning and deep learning algorithms. Various spectral indices such as Normalized difference vegetation index (NDVI), Enhanced Vegetation Index (EVI), and Satellite derived Vegetation index (SDVI) were also derived and unified into the dataset to enhance reactivity related with vegetation. The auto encoder - Light Gradient Boosting model achieved the accuracy of 0.85 outperforming the Convolutional Neural Network - Squeeze excitation model, suggesting a strong and substantial scope of nonlinear feature extraction through unsupervised learning and improvement of generalizability across various classes.

title: A Dynamic Threshold-Based Optimized SCL Decoding Algorithm for ReRAM
authors: Geyan Bao, Yanting Chen, Qiufang Lian, Miao Wu, Zijun Lu, Lin Zhou
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: Polar codes, as the first coding scheme strictly proven to achieve the Shannon capacity of binary-input discrete memoryless channels (B-DMC), possess significant application value in the field of communications. In resistive random access memory (ReRAM) channels, due to the complex channel characteristics caused by sneak paths, the fixed pruning strategy of the successive cancellation list (SCL) decoding algorithm suffers from issues such as prolonged computation time, excessive memory consumption, and limited adaptability. To address this drawback, this paper proposes an improved SCL decoding algorithm based on dynamic threshold (DT-SCL). This algorithm uses the current optimal path metric as the benchmark, dynamically adjusting the pruning threshold by combining log-domain path metric calculation, controlling the pruning intensity via a preset constant, and introducing a dual robustness mechanism consisting of minimum path protection and memory upper-limit control. Simulation results demonstrate that, under the same signal-tonoise ratio (SNR) in ReRAM channels, the DT-SCL decoding algorithm has fewer path splittings compared with the traditional SCL decoding algorithm, and reduces the decoding time while saving memory.

title: Rescearch on Charging Station Planning Based on Differential Evolution Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm Considering Electric Vehicle Travel Demand
authors: Qunying Wang, Binghui Liu, Guang Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Electrical Power Systems and Intelligent Control (EPSIC)
abstract: In order to reduce the problems such as long queuing time and too far distance from stations caused by unreasonable planning of electric vehicle charging stations, this paper analyzes the principles of the layout of charging stations, fully considers the convenience of charging users, the feasibility of planning and configuration of charging stations, and the security of the power grid, and establishes the optimization model of charging stations with the economic principle as the goal, that is, the lowest cost for the whole society. Differential evolution particle swarm optimization (DEDSO) is used to solve the optimal location and volume scheme. The feasibility and economy of the model and algorithm are verified by a numerical example.

title: BantayText: Machine Learning-Based Detection of Scam SMS in the Philippines
authors: Peter Parker, Matthew Heinz Sanchez, Neil Harry So, Luis Rafayel Jaime, Andrew Hiro Ishikawa, Norshuhani Zamin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Future Machine Learning and Data Science (FMLDS)
abstract: The proliferation of SMS-based scams, particularly those using Filipino-English (Taglish) code-switching, poses a significant threat to mobile users in the Philippines. While large language models (LLMs) can effectively identify these scams, their computational size makes them impractical for on-device deployment. This paper presents BantayText, a comparative study to identify an optimal lightweight model for this task. We evaluate four distinct approaches: a fine-tuned mBERT "teacher" model, a "student" neural network trained via knowledge distillation, an identical neural network trained from scratch, and a traditional machine learning pipeline using TF-IDF with Multinomial Naive Bayes. Our findings reveal that while distillation improves the neural network's performance, the traditional TF-IDF and Naive Bayes model achieves a superior balance, reaching a 97% accuracy and a 0.950 F1-score for scam detection. Critically, this traditional model is not only more accurate than the lightweight neural networks but also over 16 times smaller (3.5 MB vs. 58.4 MB), establishing it as the most viable solution for efficient, real-time, on-device protection.

title: Optimal Operation of Wind–Storage Systems Considering Ultra-Short-Term and Short-to-Medium-Term Power Forecasts
authors: Weizhong Bao, Liang Lv, Jinhua Li, Xinliang Wang, Yizhen Yuan, Jiabin Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: To address the economic penalties caused by power forecasting deviations in wind–storage complementary systems, this paper investigates a coordinated optimization control strategy to improve operational economy. First, an optimization model is formulated based on the Implementation Rules for Grid-connected Operation Management of Jiangsu Power System, aiming to minimize both the penalty cost induced by ultra- and medium- and short-term forecasting errors and the operating cost of energy storage. Second, a hybrid intelligent algorithm combining Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Simulated Annealing (SA) is proposed to enhance the efficiency and robustness of the energy storage dispatch strategy. On this basis, an integrated wind–storage coordinated control strategy is developed by incorporating online rolling optimization and real-time active power control. Finally, simulation studies are conducted using actual operational data from a wind farm in Jiangsu Province. The results demonstrate that the proposed optimization control strategy can significantly reduce penalty costs caused by forecasting deviations and effectively improve the overall economic performance of the system.

title: Intelligent Variable Lane Dynamic Scheduling Algorithm Based on Traffic Density Identification
authors: Wenzhi Han
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: With the continuous aggravation of urban traffic congestion, how to improve road traffic efficiency by optimizing the variable lane scheduling algorithm has become a core problem in the field of intelligent transportation. In this article, the intelligent variable lane dynamic scheduling algorithm based on traffic density identification is systematically studied. By integrating sensor technology, video image recognition technology and big data analysis technology, this article accurately identifies the traffic density, and builds a dynamic scheduling algorithm based on this, covering the overall architecture design, accurate identification module, scheduling strategy planning and operation process. The test results show that the algorithm has good feasibility and can respond quickly under different traffic flow conditions: when the traffic flow increases from 500 to 3,000 vehicles per hour, the response time of the algorithm only increases from an average of 15 seconds to about 30 seconds. Compared with the traditional fixed lane scheduling algorithm, its advantages are particularly prominent-the average hourly traffic volume increases by 20% during peak hours, and the average delay time of vehicles is shortened from 12 minutes to 8 minutes, with a decrease of 33%. This study provides a feasible technical scheme for alleviating urban traffic congestion.

title: Performance Analysis of Approximate RCA Architectures for Image Fusion
authors: N. Udaya Kumar, Bala Sindhuri Kandula, B.V.D.S. Shekhar, A. Hema, D. Madhu Latha, B. Vasanth Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Image fusion is an effective method to combine several images into a single image to extract more information. It is extensively applied in medical imaging, surveillance and remote sensing. In this work, Ripple Carry Adders (RCAs) and approximate RCA variants(ARCAs) are used for image fusion to examine the performance. The approximate adders consume less area, less power along with an inherent accuracy trade-off than traditional RCAs. This trade-off makes the design more suitable for real-time and low-power image processing applications where error resilience is of paramount importance.

title: DeepThyroScan:EfficientNetB2 Architecture for Automated Thyroid Diagnosis
authors: Shikha Khullar, Vignesh Ramamoorthy. H, Sreedhar Kollem, Selvendran Ganesan, S. Kaliappan, Indla Obulesu
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: There are thyroid diseases like hyperthyroidism and hypothyroidism that can inflict serious dysfunctions on the human body in cognition skills, or the heart and the pregnancy. Early and proper diagnosis of these diseases ensures that there will be improved patient outcome, as well as effective treatment. Standard diagnoses include imaging and blood tests among others; both of them can be both expensive and time-consuming. In the work, the application of deep learning to improve the accuracy and efficiency of the diagnostic process was proposed through a modified EfficientNetB2 model used to classify thyroid disorders automatically. The model was divided into two categories, which were healthy (0) and thyroid illness (1), and was taught on a set of 7,288 images of thyroid. The pre-processing, picture resizing, standardizing, and enriching of the dataset were used to guarantee model robustness. The EfficientNet B2 model had an excellent performance of 90 percent with precision of 0.94, recall of 0.88, and F1-score of 0.91 in the detection of thyroid abnormalities in classes 1 (thyroid illness) and class 0 (healthy). The performance of the model was determined by employing a confusion matrix, loss curves and classification criteria and this made a well-balanced strategy to reduce false positives and false negatives. Through improved access to early detection methods, promotion of health, and facilitation of the development of innovative medical solutions that would be suitable to sustainability goals, this work advances healthcare. The outcomes indicate the possibility of the instrument to identify early thyroid diseases because EfficientNetB2 is an automated medical diagnostics system that is scalable and computationally efficient.

title: A User-Feedback-Driven Dynamic Model for Translation Quality Assessment in Intelligent Human-Computer Interaction Systems
authors: Qin Wang, Yilu Tan
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Electrical, Automation and Computer Engineering (ICEACE)
abstract: This paper proposes the DQE-UF dynamic translation quality evaluation model. The model encodes source-target pairs and quantization feedback using a pre-trained cross-lingual encoder, then fuses and processes them through an attention-enhanced bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) network to output a 0-1 quality score. By continuously integrating explicit ratings, post-editing, candidate sentence selection, and implicit interaction signals, the model employs a meta-learning incremental update strategy to prevent catastrophic forgetting when adapting to new domains. Trained on 5,000 Chinese-English WMT19 segmented data and three machine translation engines, DQE-UF achieves Pearson/Spearman correlation coefficients of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 8 9 2} / \mathbf{0. 8 7 5}$</tex> with human evaluations, significantly outperforming BLEU (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.415 / 0.432$</tex>) and BERTScore (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.763 / 0.749$</tex>). Post-editing signals contributed most significantly; removing them reduced PCC to 0.870. This framework provides online self-supervision capabilities for interactive machine translation services.

title: Deep Learning-Based Paddy Crop Disease Detection Using Transfer Learning Approaches
authors: Dharun Mithul K V, Ashok A K, Mohankumar N, Prakash E.P
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: Paddy or rice is one of the most important staple food crops being consumed by more than half of the world's population. Paddy is very prone to many diseases such as bacterial leaf blight, blast, and brown spot, leading to heavy yield loss annually. Hand identification of disease is time-consuming and requires professional expertise, and therefore it is less possible for mass cultivation. For this, machine learning automation can be employed for detection and classification of diseases efficiently from paddy leaf images. In this work we widely used convolutional neural network architectures—VGG19, MobileNetV2, and Xception for their effectiveness in disease identification. Each model offers unique advantages. The proposed work aims to integrate these models into an ensemble framework to achieve enhanced accuracy and robustness. By combining predictions or feature representations from multiple architectures, the ensemble is expected to reduce misclassifications and improve generalization on unseen data. The ultimate goal is to design a practical system that not only achieves high accuracy in laboratory conditions but can also be adapted for real-time applications, providing farmers and agricultural stakeholders with an intelligent decision-support tool.

title: Harnessing Mobile Technology for the Digital Revival and Preservation of the Bidayuh Language Through BiTE
authors: Noorhayati Mohamed Noor, Siti Arpah Ahmad, Tommy Anak Peyei, Ming Leong Yii, Zarina Zainol, Zolidah Kasiran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Cognitive Systems and Computer Interaction (ICoSCI)
abstract: The Bidayuh language, spoken by Sarawak's second-largest indigenous community, is critically endangered due to urbanization creating a generational language gap. This paper presents the Bidayuh Translator Engine (BiTE), a mobile application designed to tackle this decline through digital revitalization. BiTE integrates a robust technical stack featuring Firebase, Google's ML Kit for on-device translation, and the Cloud Translation API for online functionality. The system provides a comprehensive suite of features, including bidirectional multilingual translation, an audio-visual dictionary narrated by native speakers, interactive learning modules, and gamified vocabulary exercises. The application's efficacy was validated through a multi-method evaluation. A community survey (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=35$</tex>) established a strong precedent for mobile learning, with 80 % of respondents endorsing such tools. Furthermore, BiTE underwent rigorous dual expert assessment for linguistic and cultural authenticity by a native Bidayuh community leader and for technical usability by academic experts. The technical evaluation resulted in a 100 % pass rate across all core functionalities. The results demonstrate that BiTE successfully bridges communication divides, facilitates cultural transmission, and provides an engaging pedagogical resource. This study suggests that ethically constructed, community-informed technology provides a realistic foundation for preventing language extinction. BiTE serves as a scalable and exportable model for global indigenous language preservation initiatives, effectively transforming an ancestral tongue into a dynamic digital experience for future generations.

title: Fine-Grained Vehicle Recognition in Traffic Surveillance Images via Consistency Training
authors: Xucheng Liu, Qi Zhang, Zixuan Wang, Jiangke Cheng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems (AISNS)
abstract: Fine-grained vehicle recognition for traffic surveillance is important for intelligent transportation systems, yet remains challenging due to subtle inter-class differences, unconstrained imaging conditions, and imperfect supervision arising from noisy or inconsistent annotations. To enable robust feature learning under such practical constraints, we propose a lightweight training framework with consistency regularization that enforces prediction agreement between two stochastically augmented views of the same image. The proposed approach augments standard supervised learning with a label-independent regularization term and preserves the original inference pipeline without introducing additional branches or computation. Experimental results show that the proposed method improves Top-1 accuracy by 7.8 percentage points over a supervised baseline under identical training settings. These findings demonstrate that consistency regularization can effectively enhance feature stability and generalization under imperfect supervision, providing a practical solution for fine-grained vehicle recognition in real-world traffic surveillance scenarios.

title: Multi-Stage Hybrid AI System for Employee Performance Prediction and Workforce Optimization
authors: Mubashira Fathima. A, B.N.Suresh Kumar
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: This study presents a Multi-Stage Hybrid AI System that integrates deep learning, machine learning, and intelligent decision frameworks to improve workforce optimization and employee performance prediction. Data preprocessing, feature engineering, hybrid predictive modelling, optimization algorithms, and recommendation generation are all part of the system's structured workflow. To capture both past and present employee actions, ensemble machine learning models, LSTM networks, and attentionbased processes are integrated. To efficiently allocate labour resources across operational needs, the optimization layer uses linear programming and reinforcement learning. When compared to conventional models, experimental evaluation utilizing real-world HR information shows notable gains in prediction accuracy, workforce utilization, and decision dependability. The approach decreases performance-based bias, increases resource allocation efficiency by 22%, and lowers prediction errors by 18%. This study emphasizes the value of AI-driven HR analytics and offers an adaptable framework that may assist intelligent hiring and firing, performance improvement, and strategic planning for modern businesses.

title: A Stochastic Computing-Based Architecture Design for Orthogonal Time Frequency Space Message Passing Detector
authors: Dingjie Yao, Zhisong Bie
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper reviews the application of the Message Passing Algorithm (MPA) in Orthogonal Time Frequency Space (OTFS) systems. While achieving near-optimal detection performance, its high computational complexity severely constrains practical implementation. To overcome this complexity bottleneck, we propose an innovative MPA detection architecture based on the stochastic computing paradigm. This design maps the algorithm's most computationally intensive operations to the stochastic computing domain, fully leveraging SC's unique advantages in probabilistic processing. Theoretical analysis indicates the scheme's potential for significantly reducing hardware overhead. Simulation results further verify that the proposed stochastic MPA architecture maintains performance comparable to conventional binary approaches. This work provides a promising low-complexity detection solution for OTFS systems, and its hardware-friendly features offer a new technical pathway for designing future high-mobility communication terminals, holding substantial engineering reference value.

title: From Photovoltaics to Resilience: Agent-Based Modeling of Household PV–ESS Adoption
authors: Ruihan Wang, Jumei Zhang, Chenchen Zhao
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: This study explores how neighborhood social networks influence household adoption of photovoltaic–energy storage systems (PV–ESS), aiming to bridge the gap between awareness and action in climate adaptation. An agent-based model was developed in NetLogo, where residents were embedded in a small-world network and their adoption willingness was modeled using the Theory of Planned Behavior, reinforced by peer influence. Simulation experiments tested different promotion strategies by varying network density (ties) and the ratio of strong to weak relationships. Results show that increasing connectivity exerts a stronger effect than reinforcing strong ties: adoption rose from 66% to 98% as ties expanded from 6 to 12. However, excessive strong ties reduced weak links, forming "information silos" that hindered large-scale diffusion. The findings suggest that balanced network structures are crucial for promoting PV–ESS adoption and enhancing community resilience. Policy implications highlight fostering broader exposure through neighborhood events and online forums, while leveraging trusted advocates in low-density settings. This study contributes to understanding how social structures shape technology diffusion and provides insights for accelerating the transition from cognition to action in climate adaptation.

title: A Review of Screenshot-to-Code Generation Systems
authors: Padamjit Sandhu, Pankaj Khurana, Jimmy Singla
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Communication, Computing and Electronics Systems (ICCCES)
abstract: The conversion of screenshots into source code is very important phase in the automation of user interface development. There are artificial-based tools which can understand images and further convert them into executable source code. These system's performance is further improved with the incorporation of generative artificial intelligence. These generative artificial intelligence-based systems achieve high accuracy in converting sketches into source code. Further these systems are helpful for developers, otherwise it was time consuming and costly to convert images into source code and developers often distracted from focusing on logic and functionality. This review paper presents the existing studies which generate source code from sketches using artificial intelligence and machine learning. In this paper, several studies are evaluated and compared based on different parameters. Their strengths and weaknesses are also discussed in this paper. This review paper will help the researchers and authors working in this area to further improve GUI design process and in development of more accurate systems.

title: Energy-Efficient Telehealth IoT: Enhancing Real-Time Care with Fog-Cloud Integration
authors: Yunyong Guo, Alex Kuo, Nathan Guo, Bryan Guo
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Computer and Communications (ICCC)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid fog-cloud architecture to enhance telehealth IoT systems through improved real-time analytics, data integration, and energy efficiency. By deploying adaptive machine learning at fog nodes for local data preprocessing and anomaly detection, the model reduces network congestion, lowers latency, and boosts predictive accuracy. Simulations show up to 20% energy savings, 50% latency reduction, faster processing, and improved anomaly detection accuracy (96.5% vs. 88.2%) over cloud-only setups. These results highlight the promise of distributed intelligence for scalable, secure, and responsive telehealth monitoring.

title: REST API Smart Auditing Solution: Tracking Changes and Enhancing Security
authors: Shriya Fale, Ishita Sali, Sakshi Patil, Vaidehi More, Aparna Hajare, Vaishali Salgar, Shilpa Deshpande, Prashant Katole
publish: 2025 IEEE 3rd Global Conference on Wireless Computing and Networking (GCWCN)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive solution for enhancing security and traceability in web applications by auditing mutating REST operations (PUT, POST, PATCH, DELETE). The proposed framework implements a generic filter that intercepts these operations to capture critical details such as payload, operation type, user identity (via JWT), timestamp, REST API response, and execution time. The framework extends its utility by providing peripheral tools such as a JSON diff utility and mechanisms to undo the audit records. This approach aims to improve security monitoring, troubleshooting, compliance, and testing of web applications. A demonstration is provided using an Employee Management System built with Spring Boot, Angular, and MySQL.

title: Integrating Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems to Enhance Contracting Efficiency
authors: Haiyan Sally Xie, Swetha Yallapu, Pranshoo Solanki, Catherine Shi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Social Network Systems (AISNS)
abstract: Traditional contracting processes often experience delays, miscommunication, and inefficiencies due to fragmented information and limited predictive capabilities. This paper proposes a framework to enhance transparency, accelerate decision-making, and optimize resource allocation throughout the contracting lifecycle. In this architecture, Legal-BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) extracts domain-specific semantic representations from legal documents, which are then processed by a Large Language Model for contextual reasoning and predictive analytics. Simultaneously, a Knowledge Graph organizes contractual entities into a structured relational map, enabling real-time query, compliance checks, and interpretability. The methodology combines Artificial Intelligence (AI) models for contract risk assessment with social network analysis to identify key influencers and streamline stakeholder engagement. The survey findings indicate that respondents expressed strong agreement (M=4.58, SD=1.23, and a 95% CI of [4.12, 5.04]) with the predictive analytics contributions of reduced negotiation time, improved compliance, and stronger relational governance. The paper offers practical implications for industries seeking agile and data-driven contracting solutions, supporting the emerging discourse on digital transformation in contract management.

title: Design and Control Method of Asymmetric DC-DC Converter for Efficient Interconnection of Heterogeneous HVDC
authors: Haiqing Zhang, Kang Liu, Guoliang Wu, Lijun Chen, Ke Zhao, Li Suo
publish: 2025 2nd International Seminar on Artificial Intelligence, Computer Technology and Control Engineering (ACTCE)
abstract: Under the background of global energy structure transition, flexible high-voltage direct current (HVDC) transmission has become a core technology for inter-regional energy interconnection. However, heterogeneous HVDC systems with different voltage levels and topologies present challenges in voltage matching, power flow control, and dynamic response, which traditional symmetric DC-DC converters struggle to address efficiently. To this end, this paper proposes a design and control method for an asymmetric DC-DC converter aimed at efficient interconnection of heterogeneous HVDC systems. The converter adopts a hybrid dual-active bridge (Hybrid-DAB) topology, achieving a high voltage conversion ratio and low switching stress through asymmetric configuration on the high- and low-voltage sides: a half-bridge structure with IGBT modules on the high-voltage side, a full-bridge structure with SiC MOSFETs on the low-voltage side, and a medium-frequency transformer with a 5:1 turns ratio to match heterogeneous systems such as ±500 kV and ±100 kV. A series resonant inductor is also employed to enable soft switching and suppress transient currents. The control strategy adopts a hierarchical architecture: at the lower level, a dual-loop control structure ensures output voltage fluctuation within ±0.45% via a voltage outer loop (PI controller), while the current inner loop (model predictive control, MPC) minimizes tracking errors. At the upper level, a multi-mode switching mechanism automatically selects the appropriate control mode based on operating conditions—steady-state mode (MPC + phase shift control) achieves efficiency above 98%, transient mode (sliding mode control, SMC) ensures a dynamic response time of ≤1.8 ms, and fault mode enables 100% reliable lockout. Experimental results verify a measured voltage conversion ratio of 4.98:1, full-load efficiency of 98.52%, a 40% increase in power density, and reduced switching device voltage stress to 76.2%. The converter demonstrates stable operation and rapid mode switching under various operating conditions, offering a feasible solution for efficient interconnection of heterogeneous HVDC systems.

title: New Input-to-State Stability Results by Revisiting Vector Halanay's Inequality
authors: Frederic Mazenc, Michael Malisoff
abstract: We study vector versions of Halanay's inequality. We provide an input-to-state stability result. Even in the scalar case, we provide tighter bounds than those that were available in the literature. Our key new techniques involve finding componentwise positive vectors in the null spaces of suitable matrices. These matrices are expressible as weighted sums involving the coefficient matrices that occur in the vector versions of Halanay's inequality. We allow time-varying coefficients in our Halanay decay conditions. Other key tools to obtain our tighter bounds include novel applications of the Perron-Frobenius Theorem.

title: Self-Adapting Federated Continual Learning With Gradient Causal Constraint for Fault Propagation Path Identification
authors: Kai Zhong, Xiang Peng, Zhenhua Fan, Darong Huang, Shixiang Lu
abstract: In modern industrial scenarios, federated learning (FL) has been widely adopted due to its advantages in privacy preservation and distributed modeling. However, existing FL approaches rely on a single update paradigm, which significantly hampers communication efficiency. Moreover, the server preserves historical aggregation states to model causal propagation across training rounds, instead of performing memoryless per-round aggregation. For this end, we propose a self-adapting federated continual learning with gradient causal constraint (GCC-SFCL). First, the selection of synchronous or asynchronous communication across FL rounds is reformulated as continual learning task, where an experience accumulation mechanism records historical aggregation states to model long-term temporal dependencies among client updates. Based on the aggregated context, we further design a self-adapting scheduling strategy driven by multidimensional client state information, enabling the server to dynamically select optimal aggregation. Furthermore, a causality-steering gradient constraint is introduced to explicitly regulate gradient updates, improving the interpretability of the causal model and allowing the capture of fault propagation paths. Experimental results of a simulation case and the real-world case demonstrate that GCC-SFCL significantly improves not only communication efficiency but also the accuracy of fault propagation path identification.

title: A High-sensitive and Fast-response MXene/GO Pressure Sensor for Low Pressure Sensing and Dynamic Threshold Voltage Regulation
authors: Siwei Sun, Ye Zheng, Debo Wang
abstract: In order to achieve the higher sensitivity and faster response, a MXene/GO piezoresistive pressure sensor with bilayer PDMS microstructures is proposed, in which MXene (Ti<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>T<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>) and GO dispersion are mixed in a volume ratio of 1:4. The composite exhibits a synergistic effect, ensuring high conductivity and excellent mechanical strength. Moreover, the bilayer hemisphere microstructures effectively increase the contact area between pressure-sensitive layers and establish abundant conductive paths under pressure. The measurement results show that the sensor achieves extraordinarily high sensitivity (21.65 kPa<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>) within the low-pressure range (0~1.25 kPa), remarkably fast response time (4.9 ms) and recovery time (7.3 ms), low hysteresis (3.6%), and minimal repeatability error (3.9%). Furthermore, it is innovatively integrated into the voltage comparison circuit as an adjustable resistance. By switching three loading conditions, dynamic threshold voltage regulation is completed, resulting in the output signal demonstrating different duty cycles. Therefore, this work provides a novel sensing module for voltage comparison circuits, enabling immediate and easier-to-access threshold voltage regulation.

title: Spatio-Temporal Series Prediction Based on Multi-Objective Evolutionary Deep Convolutional Echo State Networks
authors: Meiling Xu, Lixin Tang, Lu Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Accurate prediction of spatio-temporal series remains a challenging task due to complex spatial dependencies, temporal dynamics, and factors such as data sparsity, nonlinearity, and high dimensionality. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel framework which integrates a spatio-temporal interpolated deep convolutional echo state network with an improved multi-objective evolutionary algorithm. The model integrates cubic spline interpolation to enhance temporal resolution, a spatial attention mechanism to capture inter-location correlations, and a convolution-based deep echo state network to efficiently model spatio-temporal patterns. A multi-objective evolutionary algorithm is then employed to jointly optimize model hyper-parameters with two objectives of maximizing prediction accuracy and minimizing model complexity. To further improve efficiency, a topological data analysis-based strategy is used to select a representative and diverse subset of candidate solutions, reducing computational cost without compromising optimization quality. Experiments on two real-world meteorological datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves superior prediction accuracy across single and multiple horizons. Ablation study further confirms the importance of each architecture component and the effectiveness of the proposed optimization strategy. The results validate the potential of the proposed model as an efficient tool for real-world spatio-temporal forecasting tasks.

title: A Phaseless Source Reconstruction Method Based on Two-Stage Physics-Informed Neural Network
authors: Zhao Feng, Shijian Zheng, Xiaoting Yuan, Shaoqiu Xiao, Hao Chen, Yin Li
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this work, a phaseless equivalent source reconstruction method based on a two-stage physics-informed neural network (PINN) is proposed. Unlike conventional dipole-based equivalent source artificial neural networks that rely on Green's functions, the proposed approach is capable of recovering the phase information of the equivalent sources while simultaneously predicting both near-field magnitude and phase. By leveraging the physical mapping relationship between equivalent sources and near-field magnitudes, the proposed framework enables the joint reconstruction of the magnitude and phase of the equivalent sources. Moreover, through a phase-information constraint, it effectively alleviates the multi-solution problem inherent in phase retrieval. Compared with traditional iterative phase-retrieval algorithm, the proposed method exhibits superior performance in both magnitude prediction accuracy and phase recovery capability. Two numerical examples are provided to validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach. Simulation results show that for a 2×2 patch antenna array and a microstrip line on glass-base substrate, the method achieves magnitude prediction errors of 0.7% and 2%, respectively, while recovering relatively accurate phase distributions. These results indicate that the proposed method is well suited for near-field measurement, as well as antenna analysis and design applications.

title: Smart Predict and Optimize: A Tutorial on End-to-End Optimization Resource Allocation for IoT Networks
authors: Xiaoyu Song, Kunsheng Wang, Kwan-Wu Chin, Changlin Yang
abstract: The operators and nodes of Internet of things (IoT) networks often perform resource allocation, where they may solve a mathematical program to compute the optimal allocation of resources or decision(s), e.g., energy usage of devices, using sensed data as parameter values. Further, these parameter values may be stored/predicted using a neural network given one or more features, e.g., historical energy arrival rates at nodes. In this respect, to date, there are no IoT specific tutorials or works that focus on the smart predict and optimize (SPO) resource allocation framework. This paper thus fills this gap. Briefly, it shows the workflow of SPO, which involves using a machine learning model and a mathematical program to compute decisions. A key feature of SPO is that unlike prior approaches that only aim to predict the parameter values of a mathematical program, it maps the features of a system to decisions in an end-to-end manner. This paper also outlines various potential IoT applications that would benefit from SPO. It then presents a case study where SPO is used to decide task offloading from a device that has to contend with other users in a WiFi network. Lastly, it outlines some future research directions.

title: Secure performance of STAR-RIS aided networks with a full-duplex active eavesdropper
authors: Ping Yang, Liang Yang, Weichao Kuang, Shanjin Wang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Unlike prior works assuming perfect attack detection, this paper investigates the physical layer security of a simultaneous transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surface (STAR-RIS) aided network under a full-duplex active eavesdropper in the more realistic scenario where its jamming remains undetected by the legitimate transmitter. Assuming Nakagami-m fading channels, we first derive the probability density functions (PDFs) of the signal-to-interference ratios (SIRs) at both the legitimate user and the eavesdropper. Then, using the PDFs, we obtain novel closed-form expressions for key secrecy metrics, including the probability of nonzero secrecy capacity (PNSC), secrecy outage probability (SOP), and average secrecy capacity (ASC). Moreover, the asymptotic PNSC, SOP, and ASC at high signal-to noise ratio (SNR) are also derived. Based on those asymptotic expressions, we present compact expressions for several key performance indicators: 1) the secrecy diversity order and the secrecy array gain of the SOP, and 2) the high SNR slope and the high SNR power offset of the ASC. Results demonstrate that, compared to the passive eavesdropper scheme, the active jamming attack carried out by Eve will not cause any loss in secrecy diversity gain and high SNR slope, but only result in the loss of secrecy array gain and high SNR power offset. Finally, we verify our analytical results via simulations.

title: Energy-Efficient Radioisotope Identification Using Spiking Neuromorphic Systems
authors: Felix Wang, Mark Plagge, Yang Ho, Michael C. Krygier, Craig M. Vineyard, Aaron J. Hill
abstract: This work presents the development and benchmarking of energy-efficient spiking Radioisotope Identification (RIID) algorithms on neuromorphic hardware, with applications to high-performance, ultra-low-power nuclear material detection. We develop a template-based matching algorithm based on sparse coding and the Locally Competitive Algorithm (LCA) that approximates state-of-the-art RIID methods such as those found in the widely known Gamma Detector Response and Analysis Software (GADRAS). We additionally evaluate simple machine learning (ML) models for RIID which are trained through the Python RadioIsotope IDentification (PyRIID) software. These algorithms are implemented as spiking neural networks (SNNs) and deployed on neuromorphic hardware platforms, including Intel Loihi 2, SpiNNaker2, and the BrainChip Akida accelerator. To quantify performance, we evaluate these algorithms against both synthetic and real-world datasets as well as compare against traditional RIID algorithms through GADRAS. The synthetic dataset was generated through PyRIID and consists of 25 isotopes across 124 shielding configurations, and the real-world dataset consists of roughly 200 test samples spanning 7 different isotopes of interest (<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">133</sup>Ba, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">226</sup>Ra, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">235</sup>U, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">232</sup>Th, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">238</sup>U, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">239</sup>Pu, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">131</sup>I). We also measure energy efficiency of these algorithms through power profiling on the neuromorphic hardware. Significantly, our neuromorphic implementations consumed less than 1 mJ per inference, representing dramatic orders of magnitude reduction in energy consumption compared to traditional ARM-based GADRAS deployments. Our results also demonstrated the robustness of our developed LCA-based approach compared to a multi-layer perceptron ML model, which excelled on synthetic data and known noise models but failed to generalize to challenging real-world data. In contrast to the GADRAS baseline performance (F1 score) of 0.623, the ML-based model only achieved 0.466, whereas the LCA-based implementation was more closely matched at 0.579. Together, these findings underscore the potential of spiking neuromorphic hardware as a transformative technology for advancing RIID implementations, offering robust, energy-efficient, and field-deployable solutions for real-world applications such as real-time nuclear material detection.

title: Low-Carbon Scheduling for Power-Hydrogen Integrated Energy System Using Large Language Model Enhanced Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Mingde Lin, Tao Wu, Kaigui Xie
abstract: Power-hydrogen integrated energy systems (PH-IESs) are emerging as a promising solution to achieve net-zero carbon emissions. However, the large-scale integration of uncertain renewable energy sources (RESs) and flexible demand-side resources (e.g., hydrogen electrolysis) pose great challenges in the real-time low-carbon scheduling of PH-IESs. This paper proposes a novel source-demand coordinated low-carbon scheduling framework for PH-IESs, empowered by deep reinforcement learning (DRL) and large language model (LLM) techniques. The proposed framework systematically integrates source-side generation with demand-side flexible hydrogen loads to improve carbon reduction and economic efficiency. An LLM-enhanced carbon flow pattern recognition and inference mechanism is developed to enable accurate predictions of real-time carbon intensity under complex, high-dimensional operating conditions. An LLM-enhanced DRL algorithm is then proposed to directly generate optimal scheduling strategies for the flexible demand-side resources, which can overcome computational bottlenecks in real-time decision-making. Numerical simulations on two PH-IESs are conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness of integrating DRL and LLM techniques for intelligent, efficient, and low-carbon operation of PH-IESs.

title: PNProRL: Self-Supervised Neural Relighting via Photometric Perception and Progressive Optimization_supp1-3677109.pdf
authors: You Yang
abstract: Portrait relighting shows great potential in photography, film, and AR by simulating diverse lighting effects. Existing state-of-the-art methods often rely on expensive paired OLAT or synthetic data, which limits scalability. Moreover, accurately modeling the interaction between physics-guided rendering, neural rendering, and real-world remains challenging. To address these issues, we propose a novel multi-stage self-supervised relighting framework. It progressively refines intrinsic scene properties via a simple-to-complex training strategy, removing the need for expensive paired data while adapting to various lighting conditions. One core design introduces a novel pre-training method approach using diverse shading-based masking for self-reconstruction, which improves the model's perception of complex lighting variations. Furthermore, we introduce two perceptual modules that leverage the linear superposition of light to narrow the gap between physics-guided and neural rendering, and better align relit results with real-world observations. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our unified framework achieves new state-of-the-art performance in portrait relighting, surpassing recent methods in photorealism, synthesis quality, and identity preservation. It provides a practical paradigm for high-fidelity relighting under diverse lighting.

title: Dust to Tower: Prior-Driven Coarse-to-Fine Photo-Realistic Scene Reconstruction from Sparse Uncalibrated Images
authors: Xudong Cai, Yongcai Wang, Zhaoxin Fan, Haoran Deng, Shuo Wang, Wanting Li, Deying Li, Lun Luo, Minhang Wang, Hongyuan Zhang, Xuelong Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Photo-realistic scene reconstruction from sparseview, uncalibrated images is highly required in practice. Although some successes have been made, existing methods are either Sparse-View but require accurate camera parameters (i.e., intrinsic and extrinsic), or SfM-free but need densely captured images. This paper proposes Dust to Tower (D2T), a novel coarse-to-fine framework to address the coupled difficulty. The key idea is to explicitly narrow down the solution space and then introduce reliable supervision at novel viewpoints without resorting to expensive diffusion-based view synthesis. To do this, we first introduce a Coarse Construction Module (CCM) which exploits a fastMulti-View Stereo model to initialize a 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) and recover initial camera poses. To refine the 3D model at novel viewpoints, we introduce Confidence- Aware Depth Alignment (CADA), which aligns a monocular inverse-depth prior to the reliable regions of the coarse depth using DUSt3R confidence, producing sharp and scale-consistent depth maps for accurate warping. We further propose Warped Image-Guided Inpainting (WIGI), which converts the accurate warped views into multi-view-consistent pseudo supervision via elaborate warping and inpainting process. Experiments on three benchmark datasets show that D2T achieves superior novel view synthesis quality and pose accuracy over ten representative baselines, while keeping high efficiency. Code will be publicly available.

title: Global dependency modeling target identification method based on time-frequency feature constraints
authors: Xunqian Tong, Tongyu Nie, Jun Lin, Yuqi Wang, Kangcheng Bin, Xiaopu Zhang, Xintong Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: In complex interference environments, vibration sensing systems are crucial for monitoring micro-vibration anomalies, but in the case of noise interference and partial data loss, the target features will be overwhelmed, making it difficult for data-driven deep learning models to maintain stable recognition performance under multi-scene conditions. To solve this problem, we propose a time-frequency constrained global dependency (TFGD) model. The model consists of three parts. First, feature extraction is performed by convolutional neural network and long short-term memory module. Secondly, a Transformer coding module based on self-attention mechanism is introduced to establish global dependencies in feature sequences. Most importantly, to solve the key challenges of robust classification due to the lack of low-frequency information and sample class imbalance in noise and vibration data, the logarithmic power spectrum and phase spread spectrum are embedded as physical prior information loss functions. Finally, experimental evaluation of three different datasets, including noise interference scenarios and sample imbalances, showed that the recognition accuracy exceeded 95%, and the standard deviation of the accuracy was only 0.24%, confirming its effectiveness and application potential in complex environments.
